settings.title: Five Hundred Spa Days Given to Caregivers
settings.excerpt: The people who look after everyone else rarely get looked after, so we set out to change that.
settings.body:
A few years ago we started quietly offering free treatments to local caregivers, the people who spend their days tending to others. This month we passed five hundred of them.

It began with a single afternoon and one nurse who had not had an hour to herself in months. Word spread gently from there, and the number grew without us ever really announcing it.

How the afternoons work

Once a month we close an afternoon and welcome nurses, carers, and family members who look after a loved one full time. No cost, no catch, just a few hours to themselves.

The format is deliberately simple so nobody feels they have to perform gratitude:

  • A warm welcome and somewhere quiet to sit.
  • A treatment of their choosing from a short menu.
  • Time afterwards to rest, with tea, before heading back.

We keep it small on purpose. Five or six guests means each one gets real attention rather than a quick turnaround.

Passing five hundred

Five hundred afternoons is a number we never set as a goal. It simply added up, one tired person at a time, and that is the part we are proudest of.

They give so much and ask for so little. If you know someone who fits, or you are that someone, reach out and we will find them a spot.
